commit.message:
Auto-rerun analysis when UI language changes dynamically (#537)

* Initial plan

* fix: auto-rerun analysis when UI language changes dynamically

When the user changes the UI language via the AnalysisLanguagePicker while
an analysis report is already displayed, the report text was not updated
to the new language. This adds a useEffect that detects when the resolved
UI language differs from the language used for the current report and
automatically re-runs the analysis in the new language.

feat: add fetchFieldSuggestion and fieldStatus to useAISuggestions (#346)

* feat: add fetchFieldSuggestion and fieldStatus to useAISuggestions

Extends the useAISuggestions hook with:
- fetchFieldSuggestion(field, currentValue, context) for targeted single-field AI requests
- fieldStatus: Map<string, SuggestionStatus> for per-field loading/success/error tracking
- Merges per-field suggestions into existing suggestions array (non-destructive)
- Bulk fetchSuggestions continues to work unchanged

Closes theandiman/recipe-management#39

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review feedback on fetchFieldSuggestion

- Export StringFieldKey type using Exclude; restricts field param to string-valued keys only
- Clear fieldStatus when fetchSuggestions bulk-resets hook state
- Clear error at start of fetchFieldSuggestion; set error on failure (mirrors fetchSuggestions pattern)
- Filter audit trail to fieldSuggestions only (not all fetched suggestions)
- Remove field from dismissedFields on successful per-field fetch
- Fix loading-state test to properly await promise resolution

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove username from QR code payload

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Ship libbearssl.a in Linux release bundles for TLS support

Release CI now builds BearSSL from the submodule during Linux
packaging (293 .c files → libbearssl.a ~2-3MB) and injects it into
the tarball at toolchain/lib/libbearssl.a.

find_bearssl_lib() now searches:
1. deps/laststanding/bearssl/build/ (dev mode)
2. <exe-dir>/toolchain/lib/ (release bundle)
3. <exe-dir>/lib/ (minimal install)

This makes TLS work out-of-the-box on Linux after installing from
the release tarball. Windows uses SChannel (no BearSSL needed).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: add cross-platform build scripts (macOS/Linux/Windows)

Add build.sh (bash, macOS/Linux) and build.bat (Windows) that build
the specified variant (default: fossDebug), copy the APK to artifacts/,
and run unit tests. Supports fossDebug, gmsDebug, fossRelease,
gmsRelease, or 'all' to build everything.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix QR code button: use qrcodejs served locally

The previous CDN URL (qrcode@1.5.4/build/qrcode.min.js) returned 404.
Switch to qrcodejs@1.0.0 downloaded and served from /public so it works
offline and without CDN access. Update showQRCode() to use the
qrcodejs API (new QRCode(el, opts)) instead of QRCode.toCanvas().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Use plain npm in Tauri hooks and make docs cross-platform

Use plain 'npm' in src-tauri/tauri.conf.json so Tauri frontend hooks work on Windows, Linux, and macOS. Document npm.cmd as a PowerShell fallback only and update BUILDING.md and example README. Normalize Cargo.toml line endings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add QR code button to configure smartphone

Adds a 'QR Code' button in the user bar (logged-in state). Clicking it
opens a modal with a QR code encoding the server origin URL, userId,
and username as JSON — ready to scan with a notification client app.

Uses qrcode@1.5.4 from jsDelivr (client-side, no server changes).
Clicking outside the modal or the Close button dismisses it.

commit.message:
feat: fix Stage 2 fold strategy — train=90d, test=45d, slide=45d

Old: train=120d, test=60d → only 1 fold for 180d data
New: train=90d, test=45d → 2 folds for 180d, 4 folds for 270d
This ensures walk-forward validation is meaningful (3+ folds).

fix: comprehensive code review - 80+ fixes across all backend files

Critical fixes:
- Rewrite subscribe.py: generate client outbound configs (was server inbound)
- Fix UUID generation: use uuid4() instead of token_urlsafe (sing-box requirement)
- Fix Shadowsocks 2022 key: base64-encoded 16-byte key for aes-128-gcm
- Fix empty urltest crash: guard against empty outbound_tags
- Replace deprecated geoip route rules with rule_set + SRS files
- Fix transport tcp: omit transport field for TCP (sing-box default)
- Fix Docker sing-box config path: add explicit command override
- Fix systemd PATH: include full system PATH in Environment

Security fixes:
- Add auth middleware (ADMIN_API_KEY via Bearer/X-API-Key)
- Add registration secret (REGISTRATION_SECRET)
- Use hmac.compare_digest for all token comparisons (timing attack)
- Authenticate script download endpoint
- Set 0o600 permissions on agent config files
- Add CORS origin restriction support
- Add startup security warnings

New features:
- API pagination on all list endpoints (?page=&per_page=)
- Database indexes on frequently queried columns
- Protocol templates and deployment scripts
- Docker HEALTHCHECK
- Example configuration

Bug fixes:
- Fix AI proxy selector referencing non-existent outbound
- Fix naive protocol missing username field
- Fix race condition on user creation (TOCTOU)
- Validate node_ids types in batch commands
- Fix report_health missing query-param auth fallback
- Replace subprocess curl with requests in agent
- Quote shell variables in install.sh
- Fix Clash TLS gaps (trojan, tuic)
- Complete Surge config rewrite with proxy definitions

* fix: apply council fixes for pr-31-first-run-setup-wizard

- rename _secrets -> secrets in SshTunnel::connect; fail fast with
  actionable error when passphrase-protected key is used (openssh has
  no passphrase API; direct users to ssh-add instead)
- replace KnownHosts::Accept with KnownHosts::Add (TOFU: accept on
  first connect, reject if host key changes) to prevent MITM
- add TOCTOU comment on ephemeral port allocation window
- add mode guard to save_config: returns error if AppMode::Normal
  to prevent post-setup config overwrite
- add mode guard to test_connection: same guard for consistency

Fixes: SSH passphrase bug, KnownHosts MITM (high), setup endpoint
security (medium), test_connection hardening (improvement),
TOCTOU documentation (low)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(setup): 409 on mode guards, secrets cleanup on error, 409 test coverage

- test_connection and save_config now return HTTP 409 Conflict (not 200)
  when the mode guard fires (setup already complete). Return types changed
  from Json<T> to impl IntoResponse; all branches call .into_response().

- save_config steps 8 and 9 now also remove .seeki.secrets alongside
  seeki.toml when rolling back a failed write cycle, preventing stale
  secrets files from persisting on disk.

- Added two tokio tests (test_connection_rejects_when_not_in_setup_mode,
  save_config_rejects_when_not_in_setup_mode) using test_router_with_mode
  with a lazy-connected AppMode::Normal to assert HTTP 409 status.
  No live database required.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: apply third council blocking fixes for PR #31

B1: Remove hardcoded server config from SetupStep4Confirm.svelte
- Removed `server: { host: 'localhost', port: 8080 }` from the
  setupSaveConfig call body in SetupStep4Confirm.svelte:45.
- The backend's SaveServerConfig fields have serde(default) annotations
  (config.rs:96-101) that produce the correct 127.0.0.1:3141 bind address
  when the key is absent. Sending localhost:8080 was overriding those
  defaults and silently misconfiguring every wizard save.

B2: Clean up .seeki.secrets on chmod failure in setup.rs
- Added `let _ = std::fs::remove_file(".seeki.secrets");` at setup.rs:317,
  before the existing seeki.toml removal in the write_secrets_file error path.
- If set_permissions(0o600) fails after fs::write, the secrets file was
  previously left world-readable permanently. Now both files are removed
  consistently, matching the cleanup at L:329-330 and L:343-344.

Also adds third council recommendation file.

All 49 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: apply fourth council fixes for PR #31

- Add #[serde(default)] to SaveConfigRequest.server so requests that
  omit the 'server' key (as Step4Confirm now does) don't cause a 422
- Add derive(Default) to SaveServerConfig to support serde(default)
- Add password field to SshWizardConfig backend struct and wire it
  through wizard_to_ssh (fixes SSH password auth field naming)
- Fix SSH password input binding in SetupStep1Connection.svelte:
  was binding to key_passphrase, now correctly binds to password
- Add password: '' to wizardData.ssh init in SetupWizard.svelte
- Add password?: string to frontend SshWizardConfig type in types.ts
- Mark SetupSaveRequest.server optional in types.ts to match backend
- Replace no-op Drop impl in SshTunnel with a clarifying comment
  (openssh::Session's own Drop closes the ControlMaster connection)
- Add regression test: save_config_accepts_request_without_server_field

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: apply fifth council fixes for PR #31

F1/F2: Add AbortController with 60s timeout to setupTestConnection and
setupSaveConfig — SSH tunnel negotiation needs longer than the default 30s.
S1: Add 'ssh_config' to error_source union in TestConnectionResult.
S2: Remove empty Drop impl; suppress dead_code on session field (kept for RAII).

Docs versioning: Add glob support, better validation and reporting (#3278)

* Docs versioning: Add glob support, better validation and reporting

Normalize CLI target specs (handle Windows/backslashes, ./, absolute paths) and classify them as file/dir/glob. Implement matching logic and validation (report matched files and unmatched selectors, return rc=2 on unmatched), and apply target specs in scan/update. Add helpers (normalize_target_spec, compile_target_specs, validate_requested_targets, print_target_match_summary, iter_scan_candidate_paths) and adjust main argument help. Update tests to cover directory, glob, Windows-style paths, and CLI reporting.

* Handle invalid target specs in validation

Treat target specs that fail normalization as kind "invalid" so they are not silently ignored. compile_target_specs now appends invalid specs, target_spec_matches_path returns False for invalid kinds, and validate_requested_targets records invalid raw selectors as unmatched (and safely iterates when specs may be None). This ensures malformed or non-normalizable CLI selectors are reported back to the user rather than dropped.

* tests: add repo/cfg fixtures and refactor tests

Introduce shared pytest fixtures (repo, cfg) and import Callable to reduce repetition of tmp_path/git init logic across tests. Refactor many tests to use the new fixtures and ToolConfig factory, and add/adjust tests covering target validation and scanning edge cases (several validate_requested_targets variations, scan_files with invalid only-targets, and main returning 2 for invalid selector). Overall this centralizes repo/config setup and adds coverage for target handling behavior.

* Clarify --targets help text

Expand and clarify the --targets argument help strings in tools/docs_and_notebooks_check.py for the update and normalize subcommands. The updated messages document that --targets accepts exact files, directories, and glob patterns (with examples) and note that both '/' and '\\' path separators are accepted. This is a documentation-only change to improve user guidance; no functional behavior is altered.

* Update tools/docs_and_notebooks_check.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update docs_and_notebooks_check.py

* Revert "Merge branch 'cy/docs-versioning-tweaks' of https://github.com/DeepLabCut/DeepLabCut into cy/docs-versioning-tweaks"

This reverts commit 46fdb042e13eb7a968b0a1c4da0a6681cd9ef75a, reversing
changes made to 6d3b1be82255da0f832a4b47e0732507d52305d3.

* Use TypedDict for targets and simplify glob matching

Introduce TypedDict-based types for FileKind and TargetSpec (with TargetKind) and tighten type annotations across functions (compile_target_specs, target_spec_matches_path, target_matches). Replace PurePosixPath-based glob matching with fnmatch.fnmatchcase only to ensure consistent, shell-style pattern behavior across platforms and remove unused imports. Minor cleanups: import TypedDict and update variable type hints for better static checking and readability.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace 5s polling with Server-Sent Events (SSE)

- Add GET /api/notifications/stream endpoint (requireUserId) that keeps
  an SSE connection open per user with a 25s keep-alive ping
- Maintain sseClients Map (userId -> Set<res>) in server
- Add broadcastToUser() helper; call it after every mutation:
  new notification (both POST routes), delete, and action update
- Frontend: add connectSSE()/disconnectSSE() using EventSource;
  connectSSE called on login/session restore, disconnectSSE on logout
- On 'update' SSE event, call fetchNotifications() for a fresh render
- On SSE error, start a 30s fallback poll; clear it when SSE recovers
- Remove the unconditional setInterval(fetchNotifications, 5000)

Extract 3 custom hooks (useAuth, useKeyboardShortcuts, useTaskData) and
4 components (StatusBanners, WorkspaceHeader, WorkspaceToolbar,
WorkspaceContent) from the 886-line AppShell function.

behavior. Zero consumer impact — only main.tsx imports App.

commit.message:
Add Apache Struts 2 HelloWorld (struts2-core 6.8.0) (#11)

* Initial plan

* Add Apache Struts 2 HelloWorld application

- HelloAction.java: Struts 2 action with "Hello, World!" + random dice emoji
- struts.xml: action mapping for index action
- web.xml: StrutsPrepareAndExecuteFilter setup
- index.jsp: JSP template with Struts tag library
- css/style.css: centered card layout (matches spring/play projects)
- HelloActionTest.java: 3 JUnit 5 unit tests (all passing)
- pom.xml: Struts 6.4.0, log4j-core, JUnit 5, Jetty 11 Maven plugin
- README.md: Japanese docs for setup/run/test

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/kotaoue/HelloJava/sessions/15fb9e66-ffa8-4c1f-b032-23a632505fa9

Co-authored-by: kotaoue <1369749+kotaoue@users.noreply.github.com>

* Upgrade struts2-core to 6.8.0 to fix DoS vulnerabilities; align log4j-core to 2.25.1

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/kotaoue/HelloJava/sessions/15fb9e66-ffa8-4c1f-b032-23a632505fa9

Co-authored-by: kotaoue <1369749+kotaoue@users.noreply.github.com>

* Switch to javax servlet APIs and Jetty 10

Replace Jakarta EE 5 servlet/JSP artifacts with Javax equivalents and target Servlet/JSP 4.0: update pom.xml to use javax.servlet:javax.servlet-api 4.0.1 and javax.servlet.jsp:javax.servlet.jsp-api 2.3.3, and downgrade jetty-maven-plugin from 11.0.20 to 10.0.20. Update web.xml to use the Java EE namespace/schema (web-app 4.0) and tidy XML formatting. These changes restore compatibility with Servlet/JSP 4.0 containers (and Jetty 10).

---------

commit.message:
Fix weapon EXP re-bookmark dialog showing character EXP items instead of weapon EXP items (#350)

* Initial plan

* fix: use correct expItems in weapon EXP re-bookmark dialog

On the weapon detail page, the re-bookmark dialog for EXP materials was
incorrectly showing character EXP item icons and quantities because
_PartialBookmarkBottomSheet always used
assetData.characterIngredients.expItems.

Fix by:
- Adding expItems parameter to _PartialBookmarkBottomSheet
- Passing widget.expItems from showPartialBookmarkBottomSheet
- Using widget.expItems length in the onSwapExpItem callback
